The utility pole design and the general appearance of the unpaved rural road are common in Brazil. However, there are no definitive regulatory markers to distinguish it from other South American countries.
The rural infrastructure and utility pole style are consistent with Colombian rural roads. It shares similar characteristics with other Andean or South American nations.

The concrete utility pole with a specific bracket for the street lamp and the style of the concrete fence posts with wire mesh are very common in rural Brazil. The simple wooden house construction on stilts is also characteristic of various Brazilian regions.
